methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham, currently carries a $1.0B post-money valuation following $380M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables (KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Amogy at $700M, and Redwood Materials at $6.0B) alongside one public peer, with an extended competitive set of eight private frontier-tech companies in the climate and energy space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage progression, using a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C forward, implies a value of $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables yields $3.0B, reflecting the wide dispersion in current market valuations for companies at this development stage (range: $700M to $6.0B). The peer set captures material heterogeneity in capital intensity and technology maturity within the frontier-energy sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $1.4B to $3.4B, with a median implied value of $2.3B. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects substantive uncertainty around Electric Hydrogen's positioning relative to its comparables. Key sensitivities include the timeline to next funding event, technology validation progress relative to peer companies, and broader capital availability in climate-tech venture markets.
